topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara reread the manuscript under the harsh glare of her desk lamp. The words, her carefully crafted metaphors, her unique narrative voice—they were all there, rearranged, stripped of their original spark. A cold knot tightened in her stomach, spreading outwards like spilled ink. She traced a line of dialogue, the familiar rhythm suddenly feeling alien, borrowed. Her hands began to tremble, forcing her to grip the edge of the desk until her knuckles were white. How could he have done this? The realization hit her with the force of a physical blow, leaving her breathless and utterly disoriented. She pushed back from the desk, the chair scraping loudly against the hardwood floor. Silence descended, thick and suffocating, amplifying the frantic drumming of her own heart.

[story 2]

"This is impossible," Marcus whispered, the sound barely audible in the cavernous library. He stared at the printed pages spread across the mahogany table, the familiar sentences twisting into something sickeningly familiar. Every carefully placed twist, every unexpected turn of fate, belonged to him. He ran a hand through his already disheveled hair, his gaze darting around the room as if searching for an escape route. A flush crept up his neck, hot and undeniable. He felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to crumple the paper, to destroy the evidence of this profound violation. He slowly lowered his hand, his fingers hovering inches above the paper, paralyzed by the sheer audacity of the theft.

[story 3]

When the email arrived, Liam felt the blood drain from his face. The subject line was innocuous, but the content was a precise, surgical strike against his professional identity. He read the comparison, the undeniable overlap in plot structure, the almost identical phrasing of his most cherished scenes. He sat bolt upright, the cheap office chair protesting beneath his sudden rigidity. A dry, rasping sound escaped his throat. He stood up abruptly, pacing the length of his cramped apartment, his movements jerky and uncontrolled. He kept catching glimpses of the words on the screen, and a wave of intense shame washed over him, leaving him shaky and unable to focus on anything else.

"This is impossible," Marcus whispered, the sound barely audible in the cavernous library. He stared at the printed pages spread across the mahogany table, the familiar sentences twisting into something sickeningly familiar. Every carefully placed twist, every unexpected turn of fate, belonged to him. He ran a hand through his already disheveled hair, his gaze darting around the room as if searching for an escape route. When the email arrived, Liam felt the blood drain from his face. The subject line was innocuous, but the content was a precise, surgical strike against his professional identity. He read the comparison, the undeniable overlap in plot structure, the almost identical phrasing of his most cherished scenes. He sat bolt upright, the cheap office chair protesting beneath his sudden rigidity. A dry, rasping sound escaped his throat. He stood up abruptly, pacing the length of his cramped apartment, his movements jerky and uncontrolled. He kept catching glimpses of the words on the screen, and a wave of intense shame washed over him, leaving him shaky and unable to focus on anything else.
